title: Versioned Records, Field Provenance, Reconciliation, and Corrections · Goroutine Forensics
summary: A preserved Go crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, transport, body, tokenizer, goquery, frontier, goroutine, Colly callback, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first broken invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in loss-aware auditable Go record model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence de…
objectives: Version record schema, semantic meaning, missingness, invariants, serialization, and migration separately from source markup.
Attach run, URL, response, node or attribute, raw digest, transform, timestamp, and decision evidence to each admitted field.
Preserve bounded causal rejected values and counts without leaking sensitive content or silently inventing defaults.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Record Version
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 8034 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile versioned records, field lineage, rejection ledgers, counts, corrections, and deletions; every URL, body, tree, record, frontier transition, and report row retains causal identity; then change one owner, seed, net/url, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, transport, media, or charset condition and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Version record schema, semantic meaning, missingness, invariants, serialization, and migration separately from source markup.

Inspect the Record Version trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Record Version.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for version record schema, semantic meaning, missingness, invariants, serialization, and migration separately from source markup. For Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Record Version, keep Record Version connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Version record schema, semantic meaning, missingness, invariants, serialization, and migration separately from source markup. Artifact under review: loss-aware auditable Go record model.

options.text: The Record Version record says loss-aware auditable Go record model looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Record Version.

For inspect the failure record, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof. This does not satisfy Record Version for loss-aware auditable Go record model.
The record names the assumption, applies Record Version, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Correction Propagation
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-correction-propagation-1onlwb" that uses case 2034 to reconcile versioned records, field lineage, rejection ledgers, counts, corrections, and deletions. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Correction Propagation.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Correction Propagation into observable performance evidence. For Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Correction Propagation, keep Correction Propagation connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The Correction Propagation structural check is only one gate. loss-aware auditable Go record model must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
